original_abstract: The education system in Indonesia is undergoing constant transformation as it navigates the demands of a globalized era. This study explores the critical relationship between education and national competitiveness, particularly in light of Indonesia's fluctuating performance in international rankings. Through a comprehensive literature review and descriptive analysis of academic journals, policy documents, and expert perspectives, this paper identifies education as a foundational pillar for bolstering human resource quality. The findings underscore that educational equity and quality are not merely social goals but economic imperatives; they are the primary mechanisms through which individuals develop the skills, character, and innovative capacity required to compete globally. Furthermore, the article examines recent policy shifts—such as "Kampus Merdeka" and the restructuring of final project requirements—as strategic attempts to align academic outcomes with the practical needs of the labor market, ultimately positioning education as the ultimate determinant of a nation’s standing on the world stage.
humanized_abstract: The Indonesian educational system is undergoing tremendous transformation as it tries to respond to the rapidly changing demands of globalization. This paper tries to find out how education in Indonesia has an important role to develop the quality of human resources (HR) which in this case is very necessary for Indonesia to develop and increase its competitiveness. Based on the literature review, in-depth analysis of researches published in educational journals, policy and other relevant expert opinions, the findings confirm that education is a factor that underpins human resource quality development. The analysis shows that the issues of education equity and quality are not only of a social nature, but also very important for the economic interests of a country, where the skills, attitudes and creativity are needed to build the competitiveness of the countries at the global level. This paper tries to investigate the various policy developments regarding the educational system in Indonesia. In addition, through the implementation of Kampus Merdeka and reformulation of the final graduation projects, the Government expects to improve the performance of the country’s education graduates to meet the labour market demands, so as for education to be an important factor which decides the positions of a country in the global level.
